channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - wccftech.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for wccftech.com

item.title: Bungie is “Keeping a Close Eye” on UI Feedback, Request for Duo Lobbies, PC Performance and More as the Marathon Server Slam Wraps Up
item.content:encoded:
In just a few hours from the time of this writing (10am PT to be exact), the Marathon Server Slam will wrap up, and players' free weekend in Tau Ceti IV will be over. Bungie ran the server slam from February 26 to March 2, with players on PC and PS5 able to jump in and try Marathon for themselves with a few limitations.

Beyond an odd start to the slam, where the phrase 'ARC Raiders' was censored for an unknown reason, its player numbers weren't too bad, peaking at 143,621 concurrent players on Steam. What's more important for Bungie to take away has been the onslaught of feedback players have had, and as the slam wraps up, the Destiny studio has made it clear it is "keeping a close eye" on several key topics players have brought up.

One of the biggest pieces of feedback has been regarding the game's user interface (UI), which Bungie says it plans to "continue iterating post-launch" and that it wants to "make sure that you're able to easily navigate, read what's happening mid-fight, manage your equipment effectively, and see your pings without any noise around them."

Bungie is also looking at adding lobbies dedicated to players who want to run in Duos, rather than Duo teams getting mixed into lobbies with teams of three. The studio has "heard the feedback about duo dedicated lobbies," but provided no indication as to whether it'll actually happen.

PvP feedback and specifically time-to-kill (TTK) has also been a hot topic, with some players believing the TTK to be too short and wanting encounters with other players to run a little longer than they currently do. Bungie says it is "reviewing Runner density on non-beginning maps and keeping a close eye on how Perimeter and Dire Marsh feel all in all."

The studio is also looking into several issues regarding the game's PC performance, specifically an issue that's causing players to hit a frame-rate ceiling of 80 to 100 FPS no matter their hardware, frame hitches during intense fights and high CPU usage with low GPU utilization. If you are someone who experienced any or all of these issues, Bungie says that sending in a short clip of your gameplay is ideal to help the team dig into what is causing these problems.

Bungie will also continue to monitor issues like the med and ammo economy, as players have reported shield, health, and ammo packs all take up too much inventory space, only for them to be far less effective when getting into a big fight. "We hear you that meds are soaking a lot of your early loadout budget and that back-to-back fights can chew through your med and ammo reserves pretty quickly," Bungie writes.

Again, Bungie asks that if you had an issue with how the ammo and med economy operate in the game to send in some feedback so it can continue to monitor and adjust accordingly. There have already been a few issues that Bungie has acted on quickly based on Server Slam feedback, which hopefully means we'll be able to see issues like this and others fixed sooner rather than later.

Overall, it's been a positive weekend for Bungie and Marathon's server slam. There are definitely things that need to be fixed within the game, but with its release only days away, it's encouraging to see the studio step into launch with a positive outlook.

For what it's worth, I spent a few hours playing the server slam this weekend, and while I agree that its UI needs fixing both in regard to being able to read what's happening mid-fight and in terms of simple menu navigation and inventory management, Marathon has that slick Bungie quality to it with how it feels. I'll be looking forward to spending a lot more time on Tau Ceti IV when it arrives on March 5, 2026.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/bungie-keeping-a-close-eye-on-ui-feedback-duo-lobbies-pc-performance-marathon-server-slam/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: “No, This is Not The End”: NACON Connect Postponed Following NACON Filing for Insolvency
item.content:encoded:
French publisher NACON, the company behind releases like last year's Hell is Us and upcoming titles like Cthulhu: The Cosmic Abyss has had quite a wild week. A week ago today, the company announced it would be hosting a NACON Connect showcase to show off its upcoming releases and give players a tease of what's coming for 2026 and beyond.

Two days later, the company filed for insolvency following a major issue with its majority shareholder, Bigben Interactive. The future of the company and the games it is currently set to publish were put into question, and it became unclear if NACON would live to see out the remainder of 2026 with liquidation now a very real possibility if it can't resolve its financial troubles.

Today, the situation is just as unclear, but we do have an answer on one topic: NACON will be postponing its upcoming NACON Connect. In a statement shared to X (formerly Twitter), the company began by saying, "You may have seen reports about NACON's financial situation," before addressing it as "a difficult moment for us."

"Yes, things are challenging," the statement continues. "No, this is not the end."

The statement continues to explain that the company will postpone this week's event, which initially would have taken place in two days from the time of this writing, and that it will "work even harder on the launch of upcoming games and unveil the new games once our situation will be stabilized."

It adds that the release schedule for upcoming titles, which includes the aforementioned Cthulhu: The Cosmic Abyss, as well as The Mound: Omen of Cthulhu, Edge of Memories, Terminator: Survivors, Endurance Motorsport Series, Hunting Simulator 3 and Gear.Club Unlimited 3 remains "on track," and that it still has "many projects in development."

Currently, The Cosmic Abyss is the only one of NACON's unreleased titles with a specific release date, while The Mound: Omen of Cthulhu, Terminator: Survivors, and Edge of Memories all just have 2026 release windows. The rest of its announced titles do not have release windows.

More significant than the publisher's release schedule is its portfolio of studios it owns. If NACON were to be taken down the road to liquidation, the following 12 studios would be impacted:

  • Nacon Studio Milan
  • Big Ant Studios
  • Daedalic Entertainment
  • Cyanide
  • Midgar Studio
  • RaceWard Studio
  • Neopica
  • Ishtar Games
  • Crea-ture Studios
  • Eko Software
  • Kylotonn
  • Spiders

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/nacon-connect-postponed-after-nacon-filed-for-insolvency/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Starfield Reportedly Hits PS5 on April 7; Launch Price Set Lower Than Original Xbox Launch
item.content:encoded:
• 0-20%: Unlikely - Lacks credible sources
  • 21-40%: Questionable - Some concerns remain
  • 41-60%: Plausible - Reasonable evidence
  • 61-80%: Probable - Strong evidence
  • 81-100%: Highly Likely - Multiple reliable sources

Starfield has long been rumored to be set for a PlayStation 5 launch, and in the past few months, reports suggested that the latest open-world game from Bethesda would be launching on Sony's current-generation system sometime in April. Today, we learned there's a solid chance it will indeed make its debut on the system on April 7, 2026.

In a new report shared today on Dealabs, reliable leaker billbil-kun corroborated the game's release date, previously revealed by Polish website PPE, whose sources haven't always been accurate. Following their own investigation, the leaker was able to corroborate not only the release date but also confirm that the game will have a physical edition scheduled to release on the same day. Additionally, the two different editions will launch at a lower price than they did on Xbox Series X|S back in September 2023:

  • Starfield PS5 – Standard Edition: €49.99 / £44.99
  • Starfield PS5 – Premium Edition: €69.99 / £59.99

Pre-orders are supposedly starting on March 18, or the evening of March 17, but it's unknown if these dates will coincide with the official announcement of this new edition. The content of the Premium Edition has also yet to be confirmed, though it is expected to include, at the very least, the Shattered Space expansion

While Starfield's arrival on PlayStation 5 on April 7 is great news for owners of Sony's current-generation system, this new release could be good news for Xbox Series X|S and PC players as well. In the past few weeks, it was rumored that a major update introducing significant changes to the experience would launch alongside the PlayStation 5 release.

If this is indeed the case, new content should hit every version of the game in a little over a month. While this may not be enough for Bethesda to change the ultimate results of the challenging "game of expectations" Starfield has lost at launch, the improvements coming with this rumored patch could significantly improve public perception, which remains far from the universally acclaimed status that a game like Skyrim enjoys to this day.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/starfield-hits-ps5-on-april-7-lower-price/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
